title: Enable Kubernetes Audit Log Source
description:
## Enable Kubernetes Audit Log Source

Falco is capable of monitoring Kubernetes control plane events by acting as an audit log receiver. In this task, you will configure Falco's Helm installation to activate the audit log integration and specify a custom port for the webhook receiver.

**Prerequisite:** Ensure the **Falco** add-on is installed from the Add-ons catalog (🧩) before starting this task.

**Your task:**

Update the configuration of the existing Helm release named `falco` in the `falco` namespace to:
1. Enable the Kubernetes Audit Log input source (`auditLog.enabled` set to `true`).
2. Configure the audit log webhook receiver service port (`auditLog.port`) to listen on port `8765`.

```bash
# Verify your Helm release user values:
helm get values falco -n falco
```

hints.title: Upgrading Helm release
hints.body: Use `helm upgrade` with the `--reuse-values` flag to apply the new values to the existing installation:
hints.command: helm upgrade falco falcosecurity/falco --reuse-values --namespace falco --set auditLog.enabled=true --set auditLog.port=8765

commands.description: Helm release falco exists in namespace falco
commands.command: helm status falco -n falco >/dev/null && echo 'ok' || echo 'fail'

commands.description: Audit log receiver is enabled and listening on port 8765
commands.command: python3 -c 'import json, subprocess; out = subprocess.check_output(["helm", "get", "values", "falco", "-n", "falco", "-o", "json"]); data = json.loads(out); audit = data.get("auditLog", {}); print("ok" if audit.get("enabled") == True and int(audit.get("port", 0)) == 8765 else "fail")'
